helperText: The example uses a localized title and transcript so demo output follows the selected language.

productDevSprintReview.title: Product Dev Sprint Review
productDevSprintReview.transcript:
[10:00] Alex: Welcome. Today we need to review sprint 4 progress and decide what blocks Wednesday's release.
[10:02] Sarah: The auth module is complete, but MFA is still returning 502 errors on staging.
[10:04] Mike: We can fix that in around four hours if we prioritize it today.
[10:05] Nina: Browser support is still a concern. Legacy Internet Explorer support is consuming a lot of QA time.
[10:07] Alex: Let's drop IE support starting next week and inform the customer success team.
[10:09] Sarah: Agreed. For this sprint we should focus on shipping auth and the telemetry dashboard.
[10:10] Mike: I will debug the MFA 502 issue today.
[10:12] Alex: Sarah, please notify CSM about IE support ending and confirm the product owner meeting Friday at 2 PM.
[10:14] Nina: One unresolved question is whether the telemetry dashboard belongs in sprint 4 or sprint 5.
[10:15] Alex: Decision taken. Wednesday release proceeds only if MFA blocker is resolved.

infrastructureRolloutCheckpoint.title: Infrastructure Rollout Checkpoint
infrastructureRolloutCheckpoint.transcript:
[09:00] Priya: We need a final readiness check for the regional Kubernetes rollout before Monday.
[09:02] Tomas: Frankfurt is stable, but the backup cluster in Dublin has not passed failover yet.
[09:04] Elena: Security still needs the signed VPN access list from the managed hosting vendor.
[09:06] Priya: Can we get failover testing and vendor approval closed today?
[09:07] Tomas: I can run the failover exercise by 3 PM and post the results in the ops channel.
[09:09] Elena: I will escalate the VPN paperwork to the vendor account manager right after this meeting.
[09:11] Priya: Decision: we only release the rollout plan after Dublin failover passes and vendor access is approved.
[09:13] Tomas: One open risk is overnight alert noise if we leave the old node pool online during cutover.
[09:15] Priya: Add that risk to the launch checklist and assign platform on-call for Monday morning.

vendorCoordinationReview.title: Vendor Coordination Review
vendorCoordinationReview.transcript:
[14:00] Maya: We are here to align on the CRM migration timeline with the integration vendor.
[14:02] Daniel: Their latest estimate moved data mapping completion from March 18 to March 25.
[14:04] Luc: That pushes user acceptance testing into the same week as finance close, which is not workable.
[14:06] Maya: What options do we have to protect the April 1 launch target?
[14:08] Daniel: The vendor can add one extra engineer if we approve the change request by tomorrow noon.
[14:10] Luc: Finance can still support UAT on March 27 and March 28 if data mapping is frozen by March 24.
[14:12] Maya: Decision: approve the extra engineer today and require a revised delivery plan by 5 PM.
[14:14] Daniel: I will send the signed change request and ask for a daily status update until UAT starts.
[14:16] Luc: Open question: who signs off on the cutover checklist if the vendor PM is out next week?

customerEscalationTriage.title: Customer Escalation Triage
customerEscalationTriage.transcript:
[11:00] Rachel: Support escalated three enterprise tickets tied to yesterday's billing release, and we need a response plan before noon.
[11:02] Kevin: The invoice PDF service is duplicating line items for accounts with annual discounts.
[11:04] Omar: We can hotfix the rendering logic today, but we still need to backfill the incorrect invoices already sent.
[11:06] Rachel: Which customers are affected and who owns outreach?
[11:08] Kevin: Eight enterprise accounts. I will post the list in Slack within 20 minutes.
[11:10] Priyanka: Customer success can contact all eight accounts today if we provide approved language and ETA.
[11:12] Rachel: Decision: engineering ships the hotfix today, finance pauses disputed payments, and customer success sends proactive outreach by 2 PM.
[11:14] Omar: Open risk: if we reissue invoices before finance confirms tax calculations, we may create a second correction cycle.

marketingLaunchReadiness.title: Marketing Launch Readiness
marketingLaunchReadiness.transcript:
[15:00] Elena: We are four business days from the analytics add-on launch and need to confirm campaign readiness.
[15:02] Josh: Paid ads are drafted, but the product video still needs legal approval for the customer logo at the end.
[15:04] Bianca: Sales enablement is missing the one-page pricing summary, so account executives are still using an outdated deck.
[15:06] Elena: What has to be closed by Friday to launch on schedule next Tuesday?
[15:08] Josh: Legal says they can review the video by tomorrow morning if we send the final cut tonight.
[15:10] Bianca: I can finish the pricing summary today if product confirms the overage policy wording.
[15:12] Elena: Decision: send the final video cut tonight, lock the pricing summary by 4 PM today, and delay paid ads only if legal blocks the logo usage.
[15:14] Josh: I will update the campaign checklist and share final creative status at 10 AM tomorrow.
[15:16] Bianca: Open question: do we include the beta customer quote in the email launch if procurement has not approved the reference yet?

financePlanningSync.title: Finance Planning Sync
financePlanningSync.transcript:
[08:30] Martin: We need to reconcile Q2 hiring plans with the revised budget before next week's board pack is finalized.
[08:32] Sofia: Engineering requested six new roles, but the current budget only covers four unless we reduce contractor spend.
[08:34] Andrej: Two contractors on the data migration project can roll off at the end of April if the implementation stays on schedule.
[08:36] Martin: Can we commit to four hires now and hold the other two behind a milestone check?
[08:38] Sofia: Yes, if engineering agrees that the platform architect and senior analyst are the first priorities.
[08:40] Andrej: I will confirm the migration timeline by Monday so finance knows whether contractor savings are real.
[08:42] Martin: Decision: approve four hires in Q2, review two additional roles after the April milestone, and update the board pack assumptions today.
[08:44] Sofia: Open risk: currency pressure could still push software license renewals above forecast in May.
